page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage and does not feature a dedicated GPU.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng single-core CPU score and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates, which rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory performance is strong, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain average. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M parameter LLM models, though prompt processing and larger 7B model inference speeds align with average tiers.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective option for resource-dense workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. However, the shared nature of the CPU represents a potential performance tradeoff under sustained multi-core loads. The minimal 1 GB of network storage requires external storage attachments for data-heavy applications. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, memory-moderate database operations, and lightweight single-threaded processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with an x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, it is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, offering 4.0 GB of memory per core, alongside 1 GB of network-attached storage and no GPU. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded decompression speeds, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain average. The server provides a cost-effective profile for resource-dense applications, balancing high single-core efficiency against the shared CPU allocation. It is suitable for web serving, moderate database operations, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a general-purpose server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a general-purpose server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for single-core tasks and web serving.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring sixteen shared cores and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for single-threaded and web workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
1 GB network-attached storage with no integrated GPU
Best suited for web serving, moderate database tasks, and lightweight LLM text generation
